era: Hip-Hop. E=5 -- community-oriented hip-hop, self-released independence, Central District authenticity. Post-E=6 three-peat (155-157). Energy returns to sustained plateau. Twenty-fifth degree in hip-hop era (134-158).

location.formative: Central District -- historically Black neighborhood. Gentrification context. Community hip-hop roots.

members.name: Draze
members.note: Central District. Community-oriented hip-hop. Self-released. Direct lyrics over boom-bap foundations.

associated_musicians.name: Central District hip-hop community
associated_musicians.note: The broader CD hip-hop ecosystem. Community events, local venues, grassroots networks.

associated_musicians.name: Shabazz Palaces
associated_musicians.note: Different approach to Central District hip-hop: Afrofuturist vs. community-oriented.

associated_musicians.name: Blue Scholars
associated_musicians.note: South Seattle hip-hop continuity. Rainier Valley to Central District.

theory_nodes.concept: Post-three-peat transition -- E=6 three-peat (155-157) ends. E=5 returns at 158. Not decline but return to sustained plateau. The climax has passed. The era continues at its baseline energy.
examples: 155-157: E=6 three-peat. The Physics + Blue Scholars + Shabazz Palaces.
Structural: climax > resolution. Three-peat > sustained baseline.
theory_nodes.description: Post-three-peat. E=6 to E=5. Return to plateau.

theory_nodes.concept: Self-released independence -- no label, no intermediary. Direct artist-to-audience distribution. The purest DIY model in the hip-hop era. Compared to Mass Line Media (Geo, 153) and Sub Pop (Shabazz Palaces, 157).
examples: Draze: purely self-released. No label infrastructure.
Geo/Mass Line Media (153): self-built label. Infrastructure creation.
Shabazz Palaces/Sub Pop (157): major indie label. Full infrastructure.
Spectrum: self-released > indie label > established indie.

theory_nodes.concept: Central District geographic anchor -- the CD as community hub for Seattle Black hip-hop. Gentrification context. Different CD approaches: Shabazz Palaces (cosmic launch pad) vs. Draze (neighborhood presence). Cultural persistence.
examples: Shabazz Palaces (103/157): CD as Afrofuturist launch pad.
Draze (158): CD as community. Grassroots. Staying.
Gentrification: demographic change reshaping the neighborhood.
Cultural persistence: hip-hop as community preservation.
theory_nodes.description: Central District. Community anchor. Gentrification resistance.

cross_references.relationship: POST-THREE-PEAT! E=6 to E=5. Afrofuturist climax yields to community voice. Both Central District. Different approaches: cosmic vs. grassroots.
THREE-PEAT START! The Physics began the E=6 run at 155. Draze at 158 is the first post-three-peat entry.
DISTRIBUTION SPECTRUM! Geo built Mass Line Media (indie label infrastructure). Draze is purely self-released. Label vs. no label.
SOUTH SEATTLE LINEAGE! Blue Scholars from Rainier Valley. Draze from Central District. Community-oriented hip-hop continuity.
